content.text:
*[slight pause, then a small laugh]*

I mean... boredom? *[laughs again, a little more forced this time]*

No, I — look, I don't think it's fear. I think I genuinely just thrive on engagement and challenge. That's not pathology, that's just personality.

*[fidgets slightly]*

And honestly the last guy — Marcus — he wasn't even that mysterious, he was just... inconsistent. Which I kept interpreting as depth, which, in retrospect, *[eye roll]* was deeply embarrassing. He just couldn't decide what he wanted for dinner let alone what he wanted from a relationship.

*[voice picks up speed, getting more animated]*

And I spent two years trying to help him figure it out. Being patient, being understanding, making space for him. And now he's with someone who apparently requires zero emotional labor from him and they seem perfectly happy, so — *[throws hands up]* — what does that even mean? What was I doing wrong?
